hooks.command: PROMPT=$(cat | jq -r '.prompt // empty'); LEN=${#PROMPT}; if [ "$LEN" -lt 12 ]; then exit 0; fi; if echo "$PROMPT" | grep -qiE '^(yes|no|ok|sure|thanks|ty|commit|push|looks good|ship it|approved|lgtm|go ahead|do it|perfect|great|good|fine|nah|nope|cancel|stop|wait|hold on)$'; then exit 0; fi; echo '{"additionalContext": "Classify this request: if it is a feature/new work -> route to requirements-interviewer agent; if codebase question -> route to codebase-researcher; if external research -> route to deep-research-analyst; if refactor/migration -> route to refactor-orchestrator; if bug report -> use /investigate workflow and ask questions first; if user approved a plan -> use /implement with baseline verification; if ambiguous -> use AskUserQuestion to clarify before doing anything. NEVER start implementing without explicit user approval."}'

echo 'POST-COMPACT REMINDER: Auto-delegate to agents: requirements-interviewer (features/specs), codebase-researcher (repo exploration), deep-research-analyst (web research), refactor-orchestrator (large refactors), codex-challenger (validation). Codex sidekick must be consulted for all non-trivial work. NEVER implement without user approval. NEVER assume requirements — use AskUserQuestion.'
CMD=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.command // empty'); if echo "$CMD" | grep -qE 'codex([[:space:]]|$)'; then bash "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/codex-readonly-guard.sh"; else exit 0; fi

FILE=$(cat | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // empty'); [ -z "$FILE" ] && exit 0; EXT="${FILE##*.}"; case "$EXT" in ts|tsx|js|jsx|json|css|scss|md|html|yaml|yml) command -v npx >/dev/null 2>&1 && [ -f node_modules/.bin/prettier ] && npx prettier --write "$FILE" 2>/dev/null;; dart) command -v dart >/dev/null 2>&1 && dart format "$FILE" 2>/dev/null;; py) command -v black >/dev/null 2>&1 && black -q "$FILE" 2>/dev/null || (command -v ruff >/dev/null 2>&1 && ruff format "$FILE" 2>/dev/null);; go) command -v gofmt >/dev/null 2>&1 && gofmt -w "$FILE" 2>/dev/null;; rs) command -v rustfmt >/dev/null 2>&1 && rustfmt "$FILE" 2>/dev/null;; rb) command -v rubocop >/dev/null 2>&1 && rubocop -A "$FILE" 2>/dev/null;; esac; exit 0

hooks.prompt: Review the conversation so far. Was this session trivial (simple question, reading files, small typo fix) or non-trivial (feature work, refactoring, code review, architecture, spec writing, planning, research)? If non-trivial: (1) Is Codex CLI available in this project? Check if any codex-challenger agent or codex helper script was invoked. If Codex IS available and was NOT consulted, return {"ok": false, "reason": "Non-trivial work completed without Codex validation. Run a Codex challenge loop before finishing."}. If Codex is NOT available (helper scripts errored or codex CLI not installed), skip this check. (2) If code was written or files were modified, did the user explicitly approve the changes (clear 'looks good', 'approved', 'ship it', 'commit', or similar)? If not, return {"ok": false, "reason": "Changes were made but not explicitly approved by the user. Present a summary and ask for approval before finishing."}. Otherwise return {"ok": true}.
